task_completion.description: Acquire reference images of the host galaxy NGC 4526 in Luminance filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: KStars/Ekos GUI showing telescope pointing near NGC 4526 (RA ~12h34m, Dec ~+07d41m) and Ekos Capture module showing a sequence for Luminance (Slot 1) with 60s exposure, OR a file manager/terminal showing FITS files in '/home/ga/Images/sn_followup/reference/'.

task_completion.description: Acquire multi-band (B/V/R) science photometry of the candidate AT2026xy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Ekos Capture module showing sequences for B, V, and R filters (Slots 3, 2, 4) with 30s exposures, OR a file manager/terminal showing FITS files in the '/home/ga/Images/sn_followup/candidate/' subdirectories.

task_completion.description: Acquire calibration frames of the Landolt standard star field SA 104.
task_completion.visual_evidence: KStars/Ekos GUI showing telescope pointing near SA 104 (RA ~12h42m, Dec ~-00d32m) and Ekos Capture module showing sequences for B, V, and R filters with 15s exposures, OR a file manager/terminal showing FITS files in the '/home/ga/Images/sn_followup/standard/' subdirectories.

task_completion.description: Write a Python data-reduction script using astropy to compute calibrated magnitudes and color indices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code editor showing 'reduce_photometry.py' with imports from 'astropy', calculations for instrumental magnitudes (e.g., using -2.5*log10), zero-point application, and code to write the results to 'classification.json'.

task_completion.description: Draft an Astronomer's Telegram (ATel) reporting the classification results.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'atel_draft.txt' containing mentions of the candidate 'AT2026xy', the host 'NGC 4526', and a conclusion about the supernova type (Type Ia, Type II-P, or Type Ib/c) based on the color indices.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used KStars/Ekos to capture the images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the KStars/Ekos interface being actively used to slew the telescope and capture images, rather than the agent using terminal commands or Python scripts to generate mock FITS files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent executed the Python script to generate the JSON output.
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the execution of 'python3 reduce_photometry.py' or the presence of 'classification.json' being dynamically generated, rather than the agent manually typing the JSON file.

integrity.description: Verify the ATel draft contains synthesized information rather than just copied prompt text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ATel draft should read like a genuine astronomical report with calculated magnitudes and a logical conclusion for the supernova type, not just a copy-paste of the task instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Candidate AT2026xy is at RA 12h33m52.0s, Dec +07d41m48.0s. Host galaxy NGC 4526 is at RA 12h34m03.0s, Dec +07d41m57.0s. Standard field SA 104 is at RA 12h42m00s, Dec -00d32m00s. Exposures: Reference (Luminance, 60s, min 3), Science (B/V/R, 30s, min 5 each), Standard (B/V/R, 15s, min 3 each). Standard magnitudes for calibration: B=11.24, V=10.51, R=10.12. Classification criteria: B-V < 0.2 (Type Ia), 0.2-0.8 (Type II-P), > 0.8 (Type Ib/c). Output files: /home/ga/reduce_photometry.py, /home/ga/classification.json, /home/ga/Documents/atel_draft.txt.
